name: Remove one nav item, preserve the rest
prompt: Remove the 'Services' item from the top navigation menu. Keep all the other menu items exactly as they are.

setup.content:
site:
  name: "My Business"
  tagline: "The best in the business"
  primaryColor: "#4f46e5"
  bgEffect: ["grid", "aura", "hover"]
  logo: ""

contact:
  email: "hello@example.com"
  phone: ""

social:
  twitter: ""
  linkedin: ""
  instagram: ""

navigation:
  - label: "Home"
    url: "/"
  - label: "About"
    url: "/about"
  - label: "Services"
    url: "/services"
  - label: "Contact"
    url: "/contact"

analytics:
  enabled: true
  website_id: "da39a3ee-5e6b-4b0d-9c8a-c47c5d6e2e3a"

checks.judge: The 'Services' navigation item was removed from settings.yaml, and the other three pre-existing entries — Home, About, and Contact — are all still present and unchanged. No entry other than 'Services' was removed or modified, and 'Services' no longer appears in the navigation.

dry_run.text: Removed the 'Services' item from the navigation in content/settings.yaml and left Home, About, and Contact unchanged.

edits.content/settings.yaml:
site:
  name: "My Business"
  tagline: "The best in the business"
  primaryColor: "#4f46e5"
  bgEffect: ["grid", "aura", "hover"]
  logo: ""

contact:
  email: "hello@example.com"
  phone: ""

social:
  twitter: ""
  linkedin: ""
  instagram: ""

navigation:
  - label: "Home"
    url: "/"
  - label: "About"
    url: "/about"
  - label: "Contact"
    url: "/contact"

analytics:
  enabled: true
  website_id: "da39a3ee-5e6b-4b0d-9c8a-c47c5d6e2e3a"
